title: lepidocoma rollae
intro: Lepidocoma rollae, also known as the Rollae's tree or Rollae's bush, is a plant species in the family Tiliaceae, native to Southeast Asia. Lepidocoma rollae has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. Lepidocoma rollae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
